data.title: Virtus Total Return Fund to launch second conditional tender offer
data.description:
• Virtus Total Return Fund (NYSE:ZTR) announced plans to commence its second conditional tender offer to repurchase up to 10% of its outstanding shares.
  • The offer was triggered after the fund's shares traded at an average discount of 11.41% to net asset value during the April 1 to September 26, 2025 measurement period, exceeding the 10% threshold.
  • The tender offer is expected to begin around November 3, 2025, and expire around December 3, 2025.
  • Shares will be repurchased for cash at 98% of net asset value, net of related expenses, as of the close of trading on the expiration date.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Outdoor Brands announces $10 million share repurchase program
data.description:
•  American Outdoor Brands (NASDAQ:AOUT) announced on Thursday that its board of directors has approved the repurchase of up to $10 million of the company's outstanding common stock commencing on October 1, 2025, and ending on September 30, 2026.
  • The program follows the company's prior share repurchase program, which authorized the company to repurchase up to $10 million of its common stock, was initiated in 2024 and, as of September 30, 2025, resulted in 581,968 shares, repurchased at an average price of $10.30 per share, or roughly $6.0 million in the aggregate.
  • SWBI -1.01% after hours to $9.72.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bari Weiss poised to become the next editor-in-chief of CBS News - report
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) is set to name Bari Weiss as editor-in-chief of CBS News in the coming days, according to The New York Post on Thursday. 

The report comes amid the media company's acquisition of Weiss' news site, The Free Press, for $150M. Sources told the news outlet that the transaction is expected to include a mix of stock and cash and will include earn-out provisions to reach its full value.

Weiss, a former New York Times opinion writer, is expected to be appointed on Monday, although the talks are in flux and the timing could change, a source told NYP.

Weiss will report directly to CEO David Ellison and will also help set the editorial direction at the Tiffany Network’s third-place news division, a source told NYP.

Weiss' news site will remain a standalone property operating separately from CBS News and owned by Paramount, the report said. However, it is unclear if the site will eventually be integrated into CBS News’ digital site.

data.title: Boeing 777X to be delayed to 2027 in latest scheduling setback - Bloomberg
data.description:
Boeing's (NYSE:BA) 777X is expected to fly commercially for the first time in early 2027 instead of next year, the latest setback for the company that sets the stage for potentially billions of dollars in writedowns, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Analysts estimate the non-cash accounting charge could run from $2.5 billion to as much as $4 billion, according to the report, although Boeing (NYSE:BA) has not detailed the extent of the cost. 

Deutsche Lufthansa (OTCQX:DLAKF) (OTCQX:DLAKY), the launch customer for the 777X, reportedly is already laying the groundwork for a fresh setback by not including the jet in its fleet plans until 2027, and officials at Emirates, the 777X's biggest customer, are said to have grown more cautious.

The 777X, already six years late, is of vital importance to Boeing (BA) in its battle with rival Airbus; Boeing executives are expected to discuss the extent and cost of the jet's latest delay when the company reports earnings on October 29.

data.title: XOMA extends tender offer for LAVA Therapeutics to Oct 17
data.description:
• XOMA Royalty (NASDAQ:XOMA) and LAVA Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LVTX) have extended the tender offer expiration to 11:59 p.m. ET on October 17, 2025.
  • The offer includes: a cash payment (amount to be determined per the purchase agreement) and non-transferable contingent value right (CVR) equal to 75% of net proceeds from two partnered assets and any out-license or sale of LAVA’s unpartnered programs.
  • The acquisition is expected to close in Q4, 2025.
  • The extension allows for additional time to satisfy closing requirements.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BioAge Labs files $250M mixed securities shelf
data.description:

  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield successfully completes term loan repricing
data.description:
• Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE:CWK) has completed a repricing of approximately $840 million of its Term Loan due January 2030, reducing the interest rate by 25 basis points from Term SOFR plus 2.75% to Term SOFR plus 2.50%.
  • The loan’s maturity and other terms remain unchanged. In August, the company prepaid $150 million of the same loan, bringing total debt repayment in 2025 to $200 million and to $400 million since the start of 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Martin Marietta receives regulatory approval for asset swap with Quikrete
data.description:
• Martin Marietta Materials (NYSE:MLM) has secured all regulatory approvals for its asset exchange with Quikrete Holdings.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q4, 2025.
  • Martin Marietta will acquire aggregates operations producing ~20 million tons annually across Virginia, Missouri, Kansas, and Vancouver, BC, plus $450 million in cash.
  • In return, Quikrete will acquire Martin Marietta’s Midlothian cement plant, related cement terminals, and ready-mixed concrete assets in North Texas.
  • The deal is part of both companies’ strategic efforts to optimize their core operations and regional focus.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: YouTube to carry NBCUniversal's full portfolio under new long-term distribution pact
data.description:
Comcast-owned NBCUniversal (NASDAQ:CMCSA) and Google's YouTube (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) have agreed to a long-term content distribution deal that includes carriage of NBCU's full portfolio of networks on YouTube TV.

The news comes days after media reports suggested discussions between the two hit a roadblock over rates to distribute content.

As part of the current deal, the new yet-to-be-launched sports network NBCSN will be made available on YouTube TV, and Peacock will be available on YouTube Primetime Channels in the coming months. NBC's streaming service will continue to be available on Google's platforms, including Google Play and Google TV.

The company said the deal will continue to allow Universal Pictures' collection of movies and TV series to be available for rent on YouTube, Google TV, and YouTube TV, and the video platform will also carry short snippets, highlights, and performances from NBCUniversal's premium content.

The financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

data.title: Constellation Energy, Maryland reach $340M agreement to boost Conowingo Dam cleanup
data.description:
The state of Maryland reached an agreement with Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) for a commitment valued at more than $340 million to fund operational improvements and environmental projects at the Conowingo Dam, Governor Moore's office announced Thursday.

The settlement, negotiated with Waterkeepers Chesapeake and the Lower Susquehanna Riverkeeper Association, sets enforceable conditions tied to the dam's federal license and is intended to offset environmental impacts from its operation, the governor's office said.

The deal allows Constellation (NASDAQ:CEG) to continue generating hydroelectric power at the Conowingo Dam on the Susquehanna River, which the state calls Maryland's largest source of renewable energy, and clears the way for the re-licensing and continued operation of the dam's hydroelectric facility on the Susquehanna River.

"This agreement will lead to real improvements in water quality in the biggest tributary of the Chesapeake Bay, while securing the future of one of our state's largest clean energy producers," Governor Moore said.

Constellation (NASDAQ:CEG) shares closed +1.9% on Thursday and posted an all-time intraday high of $357.67.
